rationale: CCC Domain E ("Participatory Curriculum Innovation") and KMK-2.3 ("Zusammenarbeiten" / "Collaborating", using digital tools to jointly produce shared documents/resources) both concern using digital infrastructure for joint work. KMK-2.3 is a general student-facing collaboration competency; CCC Domain E is the teacher/researcher-facing analogue specific to open, version-controlled curriculum infrastructure (contributing to shared repositories, participating in professional learning communities organized around computational curriculum studies). relatedMatch: KMK-2.3's general collaborative-tool competency is a plausible precursor to CCC Domain E but does not name the open-repository/community-of-practice dimension CCC Domain E requires.
subject: CCC-v1.0.0:OE-COMPETENCY-001020
